title: Filter Rows
description: Use dplyr's filter() to extract rows based on a comparison.
problemStatement:
Tidy filtering! Given `df <- data.frame(x=1:5)`, use dplyr's `filter()` function to keep only rows where `x` is even (`x %% 2 == 0`).

**Expected Output:**
`  x` 
`1 2` 
`2 4`.
hints: `filter()` is the tidyverse equivalent of row subsetting.
solutionCode:
library(dplyr)
df <- data.frame(x=1:5)
result <- df %>% filter(x %% 2 == 0)
print(result)
